legalBasis: UK GDPR Article 6(1)(f) — Legitimate interests: public health information provision
UK GDPR Article 9(2)(j) — Scientific or research purposes in the public interest (where applicable)
dataTypes: Aggregate outbreak case reports from official public health authorities
Bibliographic metadata from peer-reviewed literature repositories
Wire service and news articles relating to hantavirus outbreaks
Ecological leading indicators (NOAA ENSO index, NASA MODIS NDVI)
Individual-level epidemiological line list data (Oxford Kraemer Lab CC0 dataset — de-identified, no names, no direct identifiers beyond nationality/age/sex)
Genomic reference sequence metadata (NCBI RefSeq accession records — no raw sequence data stored)
personalDataDetail: HORIZON does not collect, store, or process personal data about end users. The Oxford Kraemer Lab MV Hondius line list contains anonymised individual-level epidemiological records (no names, no addresses, no direct identifiers) published under CC0 by Oxford University researchers. All other case reports contain aggregate epidemiological counts. No patient-identifiable data is ingested or stored.
dataSources: THL Finland (National Institute for Health and Welfare)
Europe PMC, bioRxiv, medRxiv, Crossref, arXiv
Reuters, AP, AFP, BBC, EFE, Mercopress
NOAA Physical Sciences Laboratory (MEI.v2 ENSO index)
NASA MODIS MOD13A3 via ORNL Web Service (NDVI)
Oxford Kraemer Lab — MV Hondius ANDV individual line list (github.com/kraemer-lab/Hondius_hantavirus_h2026, CC0, Dr Moritz Kraemer / Oxford, Sam Scarpino, Andrew Rambaut / Edinburgh-Nextstrain)
NCBI RefSeq — Orthohantavirus reference genome set (HantaNet, CDC Molecular Epidemiology and Bioinformatics Team, PMC10675615)
dataRetention: Indefinite open archive under CC BY 4.0
